text: elf? Note the ringed structures along the trachea that support it and allow it to stay open. Identify any other structures along the outside of the lungs and trachea such as the pleural membrane or larynx if still attached. • Lay the lungs where they both lay flat on the table. Using the dissecting scissors make a cut along the frontal plane beginning at the top of the trachea and working your way down to the branching of the primary bronchi. • Cut along one of the bronchi, along the corresponding lung until you make a complete frontal plane cut. • Use the pins provided and label as many structures as you can identify. Your TA will come around and ask you to identify the pins you have placed. • Before leaving the station, remove all the pins you have placed. *If you are the last table to u
